steps.title: Recognize vertex form and extract the vertex
steps.content: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x-7)^2 + 3$ is in vertex form. Comparing to the standard form, we identify $a = \frac{1}{9}$, $h = 7$, and $k = 3$. The vertex is at $(h, k) = (7, 3)$.
steps.formula: $$y = a(x-h)^2 + k \text{ has vertex at } (h, k)$$

steps.title: Determine that the vertex is a minimum
steps.content: Since $a = \frac{1}{9} > 0$, the parabola opens upward. For an upward-opening parabola, the vertex represents a minimum point.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in context
steps.content: The vertex $(7, 3)$ means that at time $x = 7$ seconds, the height is $y = 3$ inches. Since the vertex is a minimum point, the y-coordinate of 3 inches represents the minimum height the ball reaches. That matches choice A — A is correct.
